title: Relations, Rows, Domains, Keys, and Missing Information · Data Contract Brief: Table Grain
summary: Support tickets, teams, members, and events must become a precise relational model without duplicate identity or invented missing values. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql table grain increment in a key-and-null-aware relational model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Declare the one-row meaning and reject mixed-grain tables before writing queries.
Represent unknown, missing, inapplicable, and not-yet-known values without substituting misleading sentinels.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Model teams, members, tickets, and events where members may be unassigned, ticket titles repeat, and one ticket has 3 events. Declare row grain, candidate keys, cardinalities, NULL meaning, dependencies, and lossless relationships. Competency probe: Declare the one-row meaning and reject mixed-grain tables before writing queries. A defensible solution applies Table Grain, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a key-and-null-aware relational model.

content.code:
-- Worked focus: sql-table-grain — Declare the one-row meaning and reject mixed-grain tables before writing queries.
SELECT t.ticket_id, t.owner_id, m.name AS owner_name
FROM tickets AS t
LEFT JOIN members AS m ON m.member_id = t.owner_id
WHERE t.owner_id IS NULL OR m.active = 1
ORDER BY t.ticket_id;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Table Grain evidence · Theory Table Grain
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-table-grain-10b52l" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; relation grain, key, reference, or NULL evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Build Table Grain evidence · Theory Table Grain.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Table Grain into observable performance evidence. For Build Table Grain evidence · Theory Table Grain, keep Table Grain connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-table-grain-10b52l
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT ticket_id, owner_id
FROM tickets
WHERE owner_id IS NULL
ORDER BY ticket_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
